title: Prediction of water stability of metal–organic frameworks using machine learning
data_sources: post-2014 validation MOFs (10 MOFs from literature)

data_scope.data_availability: fully open; datasets and code at https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4014333 under GNU GPL v3.0

core_conclusion: A machine learning classifier trained on 207 empirically measured MOF water stabilities achieves 83% weighted accuracy (two-class, Random Forest) and 71% (three-class, SVM) using only chemical features derived from the activated formula unit. Interpretable stability rules extracted from Random Forest feature importances — large metal atomic radius (>1.4 Å), low ionization potential (<7.5 eV), ligands with few six-member rings — provide actionable design guidelines. The workflow screened 88 new MOFs and is iteratively improvable as experimental data accumulate.
why_it_matters: Replaces time-intensive MOF synthesis with instant formula-unit-based prediction; provides interpretable chemical rules not just black-box scores; addresses the primary industrial commercialization barrier (water instability); generalizable pipeline applicable to any MOF design criterion; fully open data and code.
why_nature: ML resolves a genuine scientific bottleneck (water-stability screening) where experiment and simulation previously failed to deliver efficient, reliable answers; cross-disciplinary reach across computational chemistry, materials science, and ML; statistical best practices (class-weighted accuracy, learning curves, held-out validation); societal relevance to decarbonization via gas-separation and carbon-capture applications.
added_value_to_my_research: Graph-based MOF featurization via contrastive pre-training -> analogue for pre-training spatial GNNs on unlabeled mobility/POI graphs before fine-tuning on scarce health or accessibility labels
Active learning loop for MOF synthesis prioritization -> active geospatial survey design selecting which spatial units to field-verify next based on accessibility model uncertainty
Community benchmark with chemically-clustered splits -> spatial-fold cross-validation to prevent spatial-autocorrelation leakage in GeoAI reproducibility
Physics-informed generative MOF design -> encoding empirical spatial laws (gravity decay, density-scaling) as soft constraints in facility placement optimization models
Multi-task stability classification with uncertainty quantification -> multi-source spatiotemporal fusion with calibrated confidence intervals for small-area health and accessibility estimates

related.title: Molecular contrastive learning of representations via graph neural networks
related.relation: contrastive graph pre-training for molecular property prediction; directly extends the paper's formula-unit featurization to topology-aware graph embeddings

related.title: Traversing chemical space with active deep learning for low-data drug discovery
related.relation: active learning for small labeled chemical datasets; extends the paper's passive screening to targeted acquisition of most informative stability labels

related.title: A framework to evaluate machine learning crystal stability predictions
related.relation: community benchmark protocols for ML materials stability; provides the evaluation framework to rigorously characterize this paper's generalization beyond its 10-MOF held-out set

related.title: Machine learning-aided generative molecular design
related.relation: generative molecular design with ML filtering; extends this paper's stability rules from post hoc insights into physics-informed generative constraints

related.title: Deep contrastive learning of molecular conformation for efficient property prediction
related.relation: multi-task molecular property prediction with contrastive learning; extends the paper toward simultaneous prediction of water, thermal, and chemical stability with uncertainty quantification

figures.caption: Workflow for building ML models of MOF water stability — from formula unit through fingerprinting, RFE, classification training, to screening and chemical-insight extraction
Burtch dataset summary — 207 MOFs across four stability classes (S=25, HK=118, LK=42, U=22) with representative metal nodes and organic linkers
Model performance — learning curves and confusion matrices for the two-class (RF, 83% accuracy) and three-class (SVM, 71% accuracy) models
Mined chemical stability rules — metal atomic radius >1.4 A and ionization potential <7.5 eV combined with ligand MQNS features capture up to 95% of stable MOFs
